libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Prefer pure unit tests over external integration tests: exercise request builders, XML/property/header parsing, locator factories, and servlet-facing classes with local stubs or mocks. Upstream server tests mention webdav.test.url, webdav.test.username, and webdav.test.password for an external WebDAV endpoint, but those properties should not be required unless the generated tests deliberately introduce a local deterministic server.

...[truncated]

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8851 [Automation] native-image run fails for org.apache.jackrabbit:jackrabbit-webdav:2.0-beta4; label=fails-native-image-run; body_chars=8015

risks: Upstream integration-style tests can depend on a live WebDAV endpoint via webdav.test.* system properties; copying them directly would introduce nondeterminism unless a local server is provided.
The library uses old javax.servlet APIs, not jakarta.servlet; using the wrong namespace would not compile.
Servlet API versions around 2.3/2.4 are sufficient for this artifact, but local Gradle and Native Image verification remain authoritative.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: jackrabbit-webdav is a WebDAV client/server utility library whose artifact declares the Servlet API as provided; meaningful coverage of its normal server/request/response classes needs that API on the test classpath, but no external WebDAV server or Docker image is required for scaffold-level reachability coverage.
